meta.name: ESS Epworth Sleepiness Scale
meta.description: The ESS is an 8-item self-report questionnaire that measures general level of daytime sleepiness. Respondents rate the likelihood of dozing off in 8 common situations, widely used to screen for sleep disorders.
meta.author: Johns MW (1991)
meta.reference: Johns MW. A new method for measuring daytime sleepiness: the Epworth sleepiness scale. Sleep. 1991;14(6):540-545.
meta.instruction: How likely are you to doze off or fall asleep in the following situations, in contrast to feeling just tired? This refers to your usual way of life in recent times. Even if you have not done some of these things recently, try to work out how they would have affected you.

questions.text: Sitting, inactive in a public place (e.g., a theater or a meeting)
As a passenger in a car for an hour without a break
Lying down to rest in the afternoon when circumstances permit
Sitting quietly after a lunch without alcohol
In a car, while stopped for a few minutes in traffic

ranges.label: Lower Normal Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness level is within the normal range. You appear to be well-rested during the day. This indicates that your sleep quality and patterns are in good condition, allowing you to get adequate rest.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good sleep habits
Keep a regular sleep-wake schedule
Stay physically active to support sleep quality
Continue maintaining your good sleep habits
Keep a consistent sleep schedule

ranges.label: Higher Normal Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness level is average, at the higher end of the normal range. Mild sleep difficulties are quite common. Adjusting your sleep habits and environment can often bring improvement.
suggestions: Ensure you are getting 7-9 hours of sleep per night
Avoid electronic devices before bedtime
Consider whether stress or lifestyle factors affect your sleep
Consult a doctor if sleepiness affects your daily activities
Establish fixed bedtime and wake-up times

ranges.label: Mild Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is mildly elevated. There may be an issue with sleep duration or quality. At this level, sleep problems may cause daytime fatigue and affect your concentration and emotional stability.
suggestions: Evaluate your sleep duration and quality
Rule out factors that may impair sleep (caffeine, alcohol, stress)
Consider consulting a sleep specialist
Keep a sleep diary to track patterns
Consider consulting a doctor to rule out possible sleep disorders

ranges.label: Moderate Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is moderately elevated. A sleep disorder may be present, and medical evaluation is recommended. Your sleep difficulties are significant and may be considerably affecting your daytime functioning and overall health. Consulting a sleep specialist is recommended.
suggestions: Consult a sleep medicine specialist soon
A polysomnography (sleep study) may be recommended
Be cautious when driving or operating machinery
Avoid tasks requiring high alertness until evaluated
Consult a sleep specialist for a comprehensive evaluation

ranges.label: Severe Excessive Daytime Sleepiness
ranges.description: Your daytime sleepiness is severely elevated. Please seek medical evaluation to rule out sleep apnea, narcolepsy, or other sleep disorders. Your sleep difficulties are significant and may be considerably affecting your daytime functioning and overall health. Consulting a sleep specialist is recommended.
suggestions: Seek immediate medical evaluation from a sleep specialist
Undergo a comprehensive sleep assessment
Avoid driving and hazardous activities until diagnosed and treated
Inform family members of your condition for safety
Consult a sleep specialist for a comprehensive evaluation
